verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24delta1, also known as p24 family member 10) is a type I transmembrane protein resident in the CICS (COPI/COPII) vesicle trafficking machinery. It localizes predominantly to the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment (ERGIC) and Golgi apparatus, functioning as a cargo receptor in COPI/COPII vesicles cycling between ER and Golgi. Although it has a single-pass TM domain and a small cytoplasmic tail, it does not traffic to the plasma membrane under baseline conditions — it is an endomembrane-resident protein. Contextual checks: (1) no documented cancer ecto-pool; (2) no cell-death-induced PM translocation; (3) no germline/developmental surface display; (4) no activation-induced PM pool; (5) no lysosomal exocytosis route; no stable TM-partner tethering at PM. The protein cycles within the secretory pathway but does not stably reach the outer leaflet of the PM.
